description: Fix two CLI commands that display wrong port numbers when the server runs on a non-default port via --port flag

userStories.title: config show --show-secret uses wrong port in MCP client config URLs
userStories.description: As a user who started the server on a custom port, I want config show --show-secret to display correct MCP client config URLs so that I can copy-paste working configurations
acceptanceCriteria: config show --show-secret generates MCP client config URLs using resolvePort() instead of reading config.port directly
When server started with --port 9700 (port not saved in config), config show --show-secret --port 9700 shows URLs with port 9700
When port is saved in config (e.g., 8888), config show --show-secret shows URLs with port 8888 even without --port flag
ConfigShowOptions interface is not needed for the port — resolvePort(options) from the parent command's --port flag is used
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts

The bug is on line 203:
```
const port = typeof config.port === 'number' ? config.port : DEFAULT_PORT;
```

This reads the port from config.json, falling back to DEFAULT_PORT (9515). When the user starts with `opentabs start --port 9700` without saving the port to config, this always shows 9515 in the generated URLs.

Fix: Replace the above line with `resolvePort(options)` — the same helper every other command uses. This considers (in order): --port CLI flag, OPENTABS_PORT env var, config.json port, DEFAULT_PORT.

However, `handleConfigShow` currently takes `ConfigShowOptions` which only has `json` and `showSecret` fields — no `port`. The `config show` subcommand doesn't define a `--port` option either.

The fix has two parts:
1. The `config show` subcommand doesn't need its own `--port` option — Commander.js propagates the parent command's `--port` option. The parent `opentabs` command already defines `--port <number>`. When the action handler calls `options`, Commander merges parent and local options. So `options.port` should already be available if the user passes `--port`.
2. Change line 203 to use `resolvePort(options)` instead of reading from config directly. Import resolvePort is already imported (line 15).

Verify: The parent command at the top of platform/cli/src/index.ts defines `--port <number>` which Commander propagates to all subcommands. Check that `config show` action handler receives the parent's port option. If not, you may need to use `.passThroughOptions()` or read `this.parent?.opts()` — but Commander typically merges parent options by default.

Do NOT add a separate `--port` option to the `config show` subcommand — that would conflict with the parent's `--port`. Use Commander's built-in parent option inheritance.

userStories.title: config set port shows wrong 'currently running on' port
userStories.description: As a user who started the server on a custom port, I want config set port to show the correct currently-running port so that the restart message is not confusing
acceptanceCriteria: config set port message uses the actual running server port detected via resolvePort(options), not the old config port
When server runs on port 9600 via --port flag, and user runs config set port 8080 --port 9600, message says 'Server running on port 9600' (not 9515)
When server is not running, the message still says 'Restart to apply port change' (existing behavior from notifyServer warnIfNotRunning)
The notify request is sent to the correct port (the actual running port, not the old config port)
